summary: The diff implements most requirements well. The frontend covers ESP32 polling, dashboard, graphs, lifetime stats, dark theme, settings panel, and bottom navigation. The backend partially implements REQ-1 but uses separate methods instead of the specified combined interface. Several specific acceptance criteria have gaps: the backend lacks stable variables, a single getLifetimeStats/updateLifetimeStats interface, and exponential backoff is not implemented in the polling hook.

missing_requirements.requirement: Backend must use stable variables for totalFootsteps, totalEnergyGenerated, totalRuntime; provide a single getLifetimeStats query method returning all three values; provide a single updateLifetimeStats update method that increments each value; data must persist across canister upgrades.
missing_requirements.reason: backend/main.mo uses plain var declarations (not stable var), exposes three separate query methods (getTotalFootsteps, getTotalEnergy, getTotalRuntime) instead of a unified getLifetimeStats, and exposes three separate update methods instead of a single updateLifetimeStats. The absence of the stable keyword means data will not persist across canister upgrades.

missing_requirements.requirement: Implement auto-reconnect logic with exponential backoff on failure.
missing_requirements.reason: useESP32Connection.ts increments a retryCountRef on failure but does not use it to scale retry delay. The polling interval remains fixed at 1 second regardless of retry count, so no exponential backoff is implemented.

hallucinated_features.description: Internet Identity and AuthClient integration including useInternetIdentity hook, InternetIdentityProvider, and identity-aware useActor hook
hallucinated_features.reason: User authentication and Internet Identity integration is explicitly listed as a non-goal in the build request, yet a full authentication system was implemented and wraps the entire app.

hallucinated_features.description: A third CONNECTING connection status state in addition to ONLINE and OFFLINE
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request specifies only ONLINE and OFFLINE as connection states, but the implementation introduces a third CONNECTING state used during URL changes and initial load.
